title: Chrysanthemum suaveolens
intro: Chrysanthemum suaveolens, also known as sweet chrysanthemum, is a perennial plant species within the daisy family, native to East Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate fever, cough, and other respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Chrysanthemum suaveolens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Chrysanthemum suaveolens is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used to make infusions and decoctions to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, headache, and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Chrysanthemum suaveolens is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). It is a type of herbaceous perennial that is native to East Asia. This plant is also known as the Shrub Chrysanthemum or Sweet Chrysanthemum, and it is often used as an ornamental in gardens. Chrysanthemum suaveolens is classified in the binomial nomenclature as Chrysanthemum suaveolens, and its classification is further divided into several subcategories. It is a part of the genus Chrysanthemum, which includes many other species of flowering plants.
uses: Chrysanthemum suaveolens has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. It is said to possess anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which may help in treating conditions like fever, cough, and skin infections. The plant is also believed to have a calming effect on the nervous system, potentially aiding in the relief of stress and anxiety. In addition, Chrysanthemum suaveolens is sometimes used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. However, its effectiveness and safety in modern medicine have not been extensively scientifically proven.

constituents: Chrysanthemum suaveolens contains a variety of active constituents, including flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids. These compounds have been found to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The flavonoids present in this plant, such as quercetin and kaempferol, are known for their ability to scavenge free radicals and protect against oxidative stress. The saponins in Chrysanthemum suaveolens may contribute to its potential anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects. Further research is needed to fully elucidate the pharmacological properties of this plant's active constituents.

side_effects: Chrysanthemum suaveolens, also known as sweet chrysanthemum or shi chrysanthemum, is a plant species used in traditional medicine in various cultures.
